claims.claim: No thermal runaway propagation occurred in NCM622 battery modules when the SOC was below 25% in closed space.
claims.quote: no thermal runaway occurred in the battery module when the state of charge was below 25% in closed space.

claims.claim: Total gas production from NCM622 battery modules decreased from 17.48 mol to 6.17 mol when SOC was reduced from 100% to 50%.
claims.quote: The total gas production volume from 17.48 mol to 6.17 mol when the state of charge was reduced from 100% to 50%.

claims.claim: With decreasing SOC, CO2 content in thermal runaway gases increased from 38.91% to 70.45%, while CO content decreased from 23.91% to 0.06% and C2H4 content decreased from 8.92% to 1.15%.
claims.quote: as the SOC decreased, the content of CO2 increases from 38.91% to 70.45%. The content of CO and C2H4 decreased with the decrease of SOC, from 23.91% and 8.92–0.06% and 1.15% respectively.

claims.claim: When SOC decreased from 100% to 50%, the average thermal runaway propagation time between batteries in the module increased from 96.93 s to 221.88 s.
claims.quote: The average propagation times between modules at 100–50% SOC were 96.93 s, 162.85 s, and 221.88 s, respectively.

claims.claim: When SOC was higher than 50%, the explosive limit range of combustible gas generated by NCM622 battery modules was between 7.64% and 56.19%, approximately 5 times the explosive limit range of CH4.
claims.quote: when the SOC was higher than 50%, the explosive limit range of combustible gas generated by the battery module was between 7.64% and 56.19%. Comparing it with the explosive limit of flammable gas CH4, it can be found that the explosive limit range of the mixed gas generated by the module was about 5 times that of CH4

claims.claim: The TR maximum temperature of the NTM (sodium-ion) battery is 511.7°C and the maximum temperature rise rate is 2285.5°C/min, both intermediate between the LFP and NCM batteries.
claims.quote: The TR maximum temperature and the maximum temperature rise rate of the NTM battery are 511.7 ◦C and 2285.5 ◦C/min, respectively, which are intermediate between the LFP and NCM batteries.

claims.claim: Under natural convection conditions (U = 10 W m⁻² K⁻¹), a fully charged NTM battery can spontaneously ignite if the storage temperature exceeds 169.6°C.
claims.quote: Under natural convection conditions, a fully charged NTM battery can spontaneously ignite if the storage temperature exceeds 169.6 ◦C.

claims.claim: The activation energy (Ea) of the NTM battery is 0.8840 × 10⁵ J/mol, which is lower than both the LFP battery (1.0993 × 10⁵ J/mol) and the NCM battery (1.0461 × 10⁵ J/mol), indicating higher thermal reactivity for the NTM battery.
claims.quote: The values of Ea and A for the NTM battery notably undershoot those recorded for both LIBs. Specifically, the Ea of the LFP battery and NCM battery surpasses that of the NTM battery by 24.4 % and 18.3 %, respectively, suggesting a higher thermal reactivity for the NTM battery.

claims.claim: The TNT equivalent energy released during thermal runaway of the NCM battery (2.635 g TNT) is 2.2 times higher than that of the NTM battery (1.212 g TNT), while the NTM and LFP batteries (1.204 g TNT) are nearly identical.
claims.quote: the W calculated for the NTM battery, LFP battery and NCM battery are 1.212, 1.204, and 2.635 g TNT, respectively. The results indicate that the W of the NTM battery is very similar to that of the LFP battery (the former is only 0.7 % more than the latter), while the W of the NCM battery sample battery is 2.2 times higher than that of the NTM battery.

claims.claim: When the surface heat transfer coefficient U increases from 0 to 200 W m⁻² K⁻¹, the SADT of the NTM battery increases from 124.8°C to 240.1°C, showing the most significant safety improvement among the three battery types.
claims.quote: As U increases, the SADT of the three types of batteries increases, but the rate of increase, d(SADT)/dU, then decreases, and the rate of increase of the SADT of the NTM battery is always greater than that of the remaining two batteries.

claims.claim: Under N2 environment at 100% SOC, the maximum temperature of LFP-L during thermal runaway reaches 246.5°C, while LFP-SS reaches only 193.8°C, making LFP-L's highest temperature 1.27 times that of LFP-SS.
claims.quote: under the N2 environment, the highest temperature of LFP-L with 100 % SOC reaches 246.5 ◦C, whereas LFP-SS reaches only 193.8 ◦C. The highest temperature of LFP-L is 1.27 times that of LFP-SS.

claims.claim: Under 110% SOC, the highest temperature of LFP-L is 1.39 times that of LFP-SS, indicating a more pronounced temperature difference at higher SOC.
claims.quote: Under 110 % SOC, the temperature difference is even more pronounced, with the highest temperature of LFP-L being 1.39 times that of LFP-SS.

claims.claim: Under 100% SOC in air environment, the maximum pressure change during thermal runaway of LFP-SS is 0.82 kPa/Ah, while in N2 environment it is 0.62 kPa/Ah, with the higher value in air attributed to oxygen exacerbating combustion and decomposition reactions.
claims.quote: Under 100 % SOC, the maximum pressure change during TR of LFP-SS batteries in the air environment is 0.82 kPa/Ah, while is 0.62 kPa/Ah for the N2 environment. This disparity is attributed to a significant amount of oxygen in the air, exacerbating the combustion and decomposition reactions of the materials after gas ejection from the battery.

claims.claim: The gas production per Ah for LFP-SS ranges from 2.01 to 2.42 L/Ah across test conditions, while for LFP-L it ranges from 1.43 to 1.68 L/Ah, with LFP-SS consistently higher than LFP-L under all three experimental conditions.
claims.quote: The gas production per Ah for LFP-SS ranges from 2.01 to 2.42 L/Ah, while ranges from 1.43 to 1.68 L/Ah for LFP-L. It was observed that under the three experimental conditions, the gas production per Ah of LFP-SS was consistently higher than that of LFP-L.

claims.claim: At 100% SOC, both the LEL and UEL of LFP-SS are higher than those of LFP-L; however, at 110% SOC, the UEL of LFP-SS is lower than that of LFP-L, due to the change in CxHy content caused by further decomposition into low-carbon hydrocarbon compounds at higher energy content.
claims.quote: at 100 % SOC, the calculated LEL and UEL of LFP-SS are higher than those of LFP-L. However, at 110 % SOC, the calculated UEL of LFP-SS is lower than that of LFP-L. This is mainly due to the change in the content of CxHy in the gas composition of LFP-L... at 110 % SOC, due to the higher energy content of the LIB, the reactions are more intense, leading to further decomposition of CxHy into low-carbon hydrocarbon compounds such as CH4 and C2H6, thereby reducing the content of CxHy.
